VitchakornPoonyakanok

SpecialtyMD, Travel Medicine
M.S. Digital & AI Health Systems
LocationBangkok, Thailand
Status Seeking Summer 2027 Internship (USA)
Scroll
01

About

I am a Physician & Clinical Data Analyst (known as Blue) and a Travel Medicine Resident at the Institute of Preventive Medicine, Thailand. I specialize in bridging clinical practice with data architecture, having completed a Master of Science in Digital and AI Technologies in Health Systems at Chulalongkorn University and starting a Master of Science in Health Care Analytics and Information Technology at Carnegie Mellon University’s Heinz College of Information Systems and Public Policy in 2026.

Current_Focus: "National Vaccine Registry & Public Health Data Initiatives"

Seeking_Internship: "Health Care Analytics & Management · USA, any state · Summer 2027 · May 11 – Aug 20 · 300+ hrs"

→ Internship host quick-reference (PDF)

vk.poonyakanok@gmail.com
6+Years Clinical Practice4+Years Data Analysis2Publications6Live Health Projects

03

Expertise

Travel Medicine

  • Pre-travel health risk assessment & counseling
  • Immunization strategy & vaccination scheduling
  • Tropical & emerging infectious disease diagnosis
  • Malaria prophylaxis & post-travel fever evaluation
  • Altitude illness prevention & management
  • Epidemiological surveillance & outbreak response

Health Informatics

  • Clinical decision support system design
  • National vaccine registry architecture
  • Machine learning for clinical risk stratification
  • Health data interoperability
  • Digital disease surveillance & epidemic intelligence
  • NLP for clinical text mining & data extraction
03

Top Projects

Featured Work
Public Health LLM

DDS Chatbot

Guardrailed Thai disease-surveillance LLM assistant for DDC, using tool-calling over an aggregate, PII-free data mart — built to give surveillance staff safe self-service query access without exposing raw case-level records.

Live DemoLLM Tool CallingPublic HealthPDPA-Aligned
AI Clinical Decision Support

Altitude Itinerary Analyzer

AI-assisted travel medicine tool for extracting and analyzing complex itineraries for high-altitude risk assessment.

Live AppArtificial IntelligenceClinical Decision SupportTravel Medicine
Clinical Dashboard

Yellow Fever Vaccine & Malaria Prevention Dashboard

Country-specific Tableau dashboard for immunization and prophylaxis guidance based on CDC Yellow Book travel health data.

DashboardTableauCDC Yellow Book
Health Data Tool

Thai HospSearch

Navigator for Thai healthcare administrative hierarchy and facility code lookup from region to subdistrict.

Live AppHealth DataPublic Health
Clinical Planning Utility

Hajj Menstrual Delay Planner

Web utility for visualizing cycles and supporting menstrual delay or suppression planning for pilgrimage travel.

Live AppClinical ToolTravel Medicine
Tableau Public

PM2.5 & Mental Health Dynamics

Visualized spatial-temporal correlations between air pollution and psychiatric disorders using Interrupted Time Series (ITS) models.

Data VizEpidemiologyInterrupted Time Series
Kaggle Notebook

Cirrhosis Survival Prediction

Multi-class prediction model using Ensemble Learning (XGBoost/LightGBM) with engineered clinical features.

Machine LearningClinicalEnsemble
Thai Documentation Utility

Thai Numeral Converter

Automated utility for Thai government documentation, converting Arabic to Thai numerals while preserving email addresses and URLs.

UtilityLocalization
Kaggle Notebook

BNK/CGM48 4th General Election Predictive Analysis

Technical case study using end-to-end ML (Scraping to Prediction) to forecast election outcomes in the entertainment industry.

Machine LearningWeb ScrapingPrediction
View full project archive →
04

Publications

001
An Investigation of MMR‐Related Mumps Cluster Following Immunization Among Practical Nursing Students, Bangkok, Thailand, 2024
Journal of Tropical Medicine (Wiley) · Aug 1, 2025
DOI: 10.1155/jotm/9974081
Show detail

Contributors: Sethapong Lertsakulbunlue, Drunphob Srithammavong, Kamonchanok Tepsittha, Hataya Kanjanasombut, Vitchakorn Poonyakanok, Viravarn Luvira, Phimphan Pisutsan, Rachata Charoenwisedsil, Pathomthep Leowattana, Peeriya Watakulsin et al.

This study investigated a cluster of mumps-like cases among practical nursing students following MMR vaccination, identifying operational risk factors and confirming vaccine-related mumps infections through molecular analysis. Recommendations were made to improve vaccination campaign processes.

002
Preliminary investigation and rapid response to methanol intoxication outbreak linked to illegal herbal liquor in Bangkok, August 2024
Weekly Epidemiological Surveillance Report (WESR) · Sep 6, 2024
DOI: 10.59096/wesr.v55i9.3248
Show detail

Contributors: Charuttaporn Jitpeera, Watcharapol Rongdech, Kogkawee Raruenroeng, Vitchakorn Poonyakanok, Panita Kumphon, Kawinna Kerdsalung, Rittichai Jaipong, Tatcha Manta, Thanawan Gabbhirom, Somrak Sirikhetkon, Kanyarat Jarudilokkul, Kantakarn Nilsum, Thanach Photphisutthiphong, Nattinee Isarankura, Wichan Bhunyakitikorn

As of September 3, 2024, 44 individuals were investigated under methanol intoxication outbreak related to illegal herbal liquor in Bangkok, August 2024, including 20 confirmed cases, 10 probable cases, and 8 suspected cases. Eight deaths were reported, with a gender ratio of 9.25 males per 1 female, and the median age of patients was 39 years.
